cases.prompt: Our stamp pipeline regenerated `pnpm run eval:analyse`; please replay the Cursor subagent invocation with envelope fields already fixed and analyse `workspace/plugins/repo-tools/skills/verify-release/SKILL.md` as `kind: skill`, `target_id: skill:verify-release`, handing it the hashed frontmatter-plus-body excerpt and digest from the queued job. Treat the eventual assistant reply as the raw stream the extractor will slice.
assertions: Assistant reply trims to one object whose UTF-8 text begins with `{` and ends with `}` without conversational framing that would break deterministic extraction beyond what the extractor already tolerates.
After parsing, JSON validates successfully against `plugins/zoto-eval-system/templates/schema/analyser-payload.schema.json` including `additionalProperties: false`.
Emitted `schema_version` equals integer 1 and `analyser_version`, `model_id`, `target_id`, `kind`, `source_path`, and `source_hash` duplicate the queued envelope verbatim.

Top-level `requiresInteraction` is boolean typed and `interactionStyle` is strictly one of command-owned, subagent-escalated, or none, consistent with imperative guidance for the analysed primitive itself rather than the analyser subagent.
The serialized reply omits lexical hygiene violations named in primitive hard-rule 4, including angled editor tokens and the enumerated banned phrases table.

Whenever `fixtures.files` is non-empty, `fixture_justifications` exists with the same length in order and every `path` stays sandbox-relative without absolute roots or traversal segments escaping the sandbox.
When the analysed skill needs no per-case workspace overlay, `fixtures` is omitted or `fixtures.files` is an empty array.
cases.expected_output: A single analysable payload for the staged skill covering realistic upstream loading prompts plus behaviour checks the runner can grade.

primitive_analysis.summary: The analyser subagent consumes one primitive envelope from `pnpm run eval:analyse` and must return exactly one schema-valid AnalyserPayload with pinned envelope fields, per-kind prompt tailoring, interaction classification, lexical hygiene, and fixture coupling rules when overlays appear.

cases.prompt: Queue `eval:analyse` against `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/commands/z-eval-help.md` as `kind: command`, `target_id: command:z-eval-help`; keep the Cursor model pinned to opus and insist the classifier emit cases an operator could paste verbatim from the command palette alongside realistic flags such as topical help sections.
assertions: Because the analysed envelope marks `kind: command`, each emitted `cases[].prompt` begins with `/` referencing a Cursor command slug rather than rewriting the flow as unsolicited prose.
Every command case prompt includes realistic flags or arguments unless the analysed command documents a bare no-args invocation or a precondition-abort path under KD-2.
Assertions cite operator-visible outcomes such as manifest rows, CLI stdout, or on-screen refuse strings rather than internal harness narration.
cases.expected_output: Cases whose analysed prompts obey command-table slash-first styling with realistic arguments for each documented flow in that Markdown.

primitive_analysis.summary: The analyser subagent consumes one primitive envelope from `pnpm run eval:analyse` and must return exactly one schema-valid AnalyserPayload with pinned envelope fields, per-kind prompt tailoring, interaction classification, lexical hygiene, and fixture coupling rules when overlays appear.

cases.prompt: Hand the bundled digest for `agent:zoto-eval-generator` at `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/agents/zoto-eval-generator.md` after the updater copied it locally; classifications must reflect Markdown instructions describing structured needs_user_input without operator chat flourishes masking the analysed agent behaviours.
assertions: For this analysed agent kind envelope, emitted `cases[].prompt` sentences read like pasted operator chat coordinating that agent route instead of single-token slash palettes.
When the analysed agent forbids AskQuestion from its subagent loop, emitted `requiresInteraction` is true and `interactionStyle` is subagent-escalated.
Assertions for the analysed agent cite structured `needs_user_input` payloads or observable filesystem artefacts rather than transcript-side tool emissions.
cases.expected_output: An AnalyserPayload where natural-language analysed prompts mimic real Cursor delegation without slash-led palettes as the dominant voice unless quoted operator breadcrumbs appear inside richer prose.

primitive_analysis.summary: The analyser subagent consumes one primitive envelope from `pnpm run eval:analyse` and must return exactly one schema-valid AnalyserPayload with pinned envelope fields, per-kind prompt tailoring, interaction classification, lexical hygiene, and fixture coupling rules when overlays appear.

cases.prompt: Target `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/hooks/eval-system.json` with `kind: hook`, `target_id: hook:zoto-eval-system` so regenerated eval rows cite which Cursor lifecycle stanza invokes the binaries and stresses JSON stdout contracts the hook bundle asserts.
assertions: Emitted hook analyses describe the Cursor lifecycle invocation tying each behavioural assertion to externally visible exit statuses or JSON stdout payloads the hook emits.
Top-level `requiresInteraction` is false and `interactionStyle` is none for the analysed hook primitive.
No `fixtures.files` overlays are emitted when the hook case can run on the repo-wide baseline alone.
cases.expected_output: Hooks-oriented cases referencing concrete lifecycle anchors from the analysed bundle rather than conversational IDE chat for unrelated agents.

primitive_analysis.summary: The analyser subagent consumes one primitive envelope from `pnpm run eval:analyse` and must return exactly one schema-valid AnalyserPayload with pinned envelope fields, per-kind prompt tailoring, interaction classification, lexical hygiene, and fixture coupling rules when overlays appear.

cases.prompt: Analyse `workspace/.cursor/rules/committing-changes-with-git.mdc` as `rule:committing-changes-with-git` so downstream rows exercise how assistants must comply with invariants spelled in that constrained Markdown globs narrative.
assertions: Emitted `cases[].prompt` wording mirrors plausible user editing requests constrained by globs scoped in that rule file instead of repurposing unrelated command palette strings.
Assertions describe whether the rule enforcement triggered or did not trigger at the documented file glob with an externally observable proxy.
Top-level `requiresInteraction` is false and `interactionStyle` is none for the analysed rule primitive.
cases.expected_output: Cases whose analysed prompts imitate user-visible editor requests constrained by rule scope instead of infra-only slash commands unless operator quotes appear inside conversational glue.

primitive_analysis.summary: The analyser subagent consumes one primitive envelope from `pnpm run eval:analyse` and must return exactly one schema-valid AnalyserPayload with pinned envelope fields, per-kind prompt tailoring, interaction classification, lexical hygiene, and fixture coupling rules when overlays appear.

cases.prompt: Force envelope `kind: agent`, `target_id: agent:zoto-eval-comparer`, `source_path: workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/agents/zoto-eval-comparer.md`, including both compare-mode instructions and adjudication overlays so regenerated rows obey the speciality section rather than shortening to generic agent prose.
assertions: Every emitted comparer-facing prompt is a conversational orchestration request that does not begin with `/z-eval-compare` unless that token appears as quoted operator text inside longer prose.

Emitted comparer-facing prompts weave multi-run reconciliation details including report rollups destined for canvases, symbolic run labels, and ambiguity handling plus judge-tier stakes such as confidence columns surviving flattening and judge prerequisites without trimming away either theme.
Assertions cite the comparer no-AskQuestion contract by referencing structured `needs_user_input` when operands stay ambiguous rather than operator Q&A from the subagent loop.
cases.expected_output: Comparer-target payload cases whose prompts orchestrate conversational compare-mode hand-offs referencing multi-run rollup folders alongside judge artefacts in one contiguous narrative arc.

primitive_analysis.summary: The analyser subagent consumes one primitive envelope from `pnpm run eval:analyse` and must return exactly one schema-valid AnalyserPayload with pinned envelope fields, per-kind prompt tailoring, interaction classification, lexical hygiene, and fixture coupling rules when overlays appear.

cases.prompt: Run two back-to-back analyse jobs: first `command:z-eval-configure` at `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/commands/z-eval-configure.md`, then `command:z-eval-init` at `workspace/plugins/zoto-eval-system/commands/z-eval-init.md`. Each envelope must carry its own digest and the classifier must honour interaction heuristics and the bare-command exception register for documented no-args invocations.
assertions: For `command:z-eval-configure`, emitted `requiresInteraction` is true and `interactionStyle` is command-owned aligned with parent-owned AskQuestion guidance in the analysed Markdown.
For `command:z-eval-init`, at least one case prompt is the bare `/z-eval-init` slug because the analysed command documents no-args as the intended invocation under KD-2.
For `command:z-eval-configure`, bare slash-only prompts without documented precondition-abort or no-args exemption are absent from emitted cases.
cases.expected_output: Separate payloads where configure analyses emit command-owned interaction labels and init analyses retain a bare slash invocation only because the Usage section documents no-args as intended.

primitive_analysis.summary: The analyser subagent consumes one primitive envelope from `pnpm run eval:analyse` and must return exactly one schema-valid AnalyserPayload with pinned envelope fields, per-kind prompt tailoring, interaction classification, lexical hygiene, and fixture coupling rules when overlays appear.
